This is a common symptom of incorrect handling of data dependencies, especially in loops.

The provided reproducer contains a loop with a recurrence. A value computed in the loop (`%mul37`) is fed back to a PHI node (`%phi`) at the loop header. This creates a loop-carried dependency. The SLP vectorizer needs to be careful not to break such dependency chains.

The backtrace shows the crash happens during the cleanup phase of the SLP vectorizer, specifically when destroying a `BoUpSLP` object. This object holds a list of `VectorizedInstructions` that are supposed to be safe to delete after vectorization. It adds a check: if a user of a scalar has the same opcode as the recurrence PHI node and resides in the same loop, it's likely part of the recurrence's computation chain. Such an instruction should not be treated as a simple external use, so it's skipped.
